channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PM Modi urges prudent use of fuel to reduce impact of Middle East conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, during an event in Hyderabad, emphasised the need for Indians to use petrol, diesel, and gas judiciously amid the ongoing Middle East conflict. Highlighting the importanc...

item.title: Federal government delays national restrictions on vaping flavours despite expert calls to protect youth
item.description: Five years after the federal Liberals first promised in 2021 to restrict vaping flavours across Canada to only mint, menthol, and tobacco, the government has yet to implement the measure. Health Minis...

item.title: President Tinubu Praises Fasoranti on His 100th Birthday for Lifelong Service and Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don celebrate elder statesman and Afenifere leader, Pa Reuben Fasoranti, as e reach 100 years on May 11, 2026. Tinubu tok say Fasoranti na model of integrity and service, and e l...

item.title: Kenya Airways Flight Disruptions After Bird Strike in Cape Town
item.description: Kenya Airways has reported flight disruptions following a bird strike incident at Cape Town International Airport in South Africa. On arrival from Nairobi, one of KQ's aircraft experienced a bird stri...

item.title: Jorge Martin claims French MotoGP win as Aprilia achieves historic podium sweep
item.description: At the French Grand Prix on Sunday, Aprilia’s Jorge Martin delivered an impressive performance by winning the race from seventh on the grid. This marks Martin’s first MotoGP victory since his champion...

item.title: Labour Faces Internal Pressure After Local Election Losses
item.description: Following a significant defeat in recent local elections, Sir Keir Starmer, the Prime Minister, is facing mounting criticism from within the Labour Party. Union leaders, including Sharon Graham of Uni...

item.title: Court Confirms BAKWATA as Lawful Administrator of Bangulo Mosque
item.description: Dar es Salaam, Tanzania – The High Court of Tanzania, Land Division, has officially ruled that the Registered Trustees of the National Muslim Council of Tanzania (BAKWATA) in Ilala District are the la...

item.title: Haryana Board Class 10 and 12 Results 2026: Access Details and Previous Years’ Trends
item.description: The official Haryana Board of School Education (BSEH) website is currently denying access to the page for the 2026 board results for Class 10 and 12. Users attempting to check their results have been ...

item.title: Understanding Hyperpolitics in the Post-Politics Era
item.description: In this thoughtful piece, Nadeem F. Paracha explores the concept of hyperpolitics coined by Belgian theorist Anton Jäger. He explains how 20th century mass politics, driven by strong institutions, uni...

item.title: Nairobi AI Summit Seen as Opportunity for Tanzania’s Tech Growth and Regional Partnerships
item.description: Tanzania is expected to benefit from the upcoming AI Everything Kenya X Gitex Kenya conference, which will take place in Nairobi from May 19 to 21, 2026. The event will bring together technology leade...

item.title: Opinion piece argues unresolved geopolitical grievances prolong Ukraine and Iran conflicts
item.description: The article presents an opinionated argument that ongoing conflicts involving Ukraine and Iran cannot be resolved without addressing their underlying geopolitical causes. The author claims that Russia...

item.title: Supreme Court Reverses Carandang’s Illegal Dismissal, Upholding Ombudsman Independence
item.description: Melchor Arthur Carandang, former overall deputy ombudsman, was finally vindicated seven years after his illegal dismissal during the Duterte administration. Carandang had led the Ombudsman probe into ...

item.title: Alberta Separatism Gains Mainstream Traction After 2025 Election
item.description: Alberta separatism, a long-standing undercurrent of provincial alienation from Ottawa, has surged into mainstream discourse following the April 2025 federal election re-election of the Liberal governm...

item.title: PNP reforms and increased police presence boost public trust and reduce crime
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has reported that public trust and satisfaction in the Philippine National Police (PNP) have significantly improved, reflecting a growing sen...

item.title: PTCL announces scheduled submarine cable maintenance affecting internet speeds
item.description: Pakistan Telecommunication Company Limited (PTCL) has announced that internet users across Pakistan may face slower connectivity due to planned maintenance on one of its submarine cables. The maintena...

item.title: Towards a Sustainable African Migration Policy
item.description: Migration across Africa is a growing and complex challenge, driven by conflict, unemployment, inequality, political instability, climate pressures, and the aspiration for a better life. While migratio...

item.title: Creating Safer and More Useful Digital Town Squares
item.description: The concept of a digital town square, once symbolized by platforms like Twitter (now X), has evolved from centralized spaces owned by big tech companies to more community-driven initiatives. Previousl...

item.title: Evacuation of Cruise Ship Passengers Following Hantavirus Cases
item.description: Spain has started evacuating its nationals from a cruise ship that recently reported a hantavirus outbreak, anchored near Tenerife. Spanish passengers are being brought ashore in small groups, taken t...

item.title: AA Driving School: Branches, Fees, and Training Programmes Across Kenya
item.description: AA Driving School is one of the most trusted driving schools in Kenya, offering professional training across a wide network of branches in Nairobi, Mombasa, Thika, and many other towns. Learners can a...

item.title: Ontario police investigate high school game after water gun incident involving unhoused man
item.description: Police in Ontario are investigating an incident linked to the popular 'Senior Assassin' game, which has recently gained traction among Grade 12 students. The game, often played as a stress-relief acti...
